goal.answer: I am building a Python terminal snake-game engine library named `snakelib`, implementing all game rules and features as specified, with accompanying tests and a renderer.

module.answer: The package/module must be named exactly `snakelib`.

grid.answer: The grid is exactly 17 columns by 23 rows.

apple.answer: Eating a normal apple awards exactly 7 points.

golden.answer: Every 5th apple eaten is a golden apple and it awards 21 points instead of 7.

walls.answer: The snake wraps around the wall edges due to torus topology; it never dies from hitting a wall, only on self-collision.

seed.answer: The RNG must use the default seed 4242.

speed.answer: The game speed increases by 4% per apple eaten, meaning the tick duration decreases by 4% each time; the starting tick value is 250 milliseconds.

api.answer: The public API entrypoint class is named `GameEngine` and it is in the file `snakelib/engine.py`.

start.answer: The snake starts with length 3, centered in the grid, moving EAST. The initial snake coordinates are the three cells centered horizontally at the middle row, with the head at the middle cell.

My immediate next step is to run pytest on the engine tests, fix any failing tests, and iterate until all pass before moving to the next deliverable.
